steps.title: Rewrite the given equation
steps.content: The equation $\left(\dfrac{1}{2}x - \dfrac{37}{2}\right)^2 - 81 = 0$ can be written as $\left(\dfrac{x-37}{2}\right)^2 = 81$, since $\dfrac{1}{2}x - \dfrac{37}{2} = \dfrac{x-37}{2}$.

steps.title: Isolate $(x-37)^2
steps.content:
Multiply both sides by $4$: $(x-37)^2 = 4 \cdot 81 = 324$.

  • Choice A — $324$: matches $(x-37)^2 = 324$. ✓
  • Choice B — $162$: would require multiplying by $2$, not $4$. ✗
  • Choice C — $37$: confuses the constant in the expression for the answer. ✗
  • Choice D — $9$: confuses $\sqrt{81}$ for the answer instead of $4 \times 81$. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
